source.title: Understanding depressive rumination from a cognitive science perspective: the impaired disengagement hypothesis

confidence_rationale: Canonical mechanistic statement that depressive rumination is a FAILURE of the disengage operation itself (impaired attentional disengagement / weak conflict-control), not an over-strong persistence drive. Strong support that failure-to-disengage is a distinct, separable pathology -> REE needs an explicit abandon trigger, not just tuned persistence weights. Held at 0.75 because the disengagement is attentional/working-memory level, not goal-commitment-level.
failure_signatures: Negative self-referential content enters the focus of processing and the system cannot relinquish it -> a self-sustaining re-elaboration loop with no terminating condition.
Attempts to switch to a neutral/goal-relevant task are repeatedly overridden by re-capture of attention by the negative content.
The loop persists DESPITE being recognised as unproductive -- the persistence is not endorsed by a higher controller; it reflects an ABSENT disengage signal, not strong goal value.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Depressive rumination is not driven by an over-strong self-focus drive but by impaired disengagement: a failure of attentional/executive control to remove negative self-referent material from the focus of working memory and shift elsewhere once it has captured processing.
mapping.ree_translation: Strong support for ARC-078 C3's premise that failure-to-disengage is a distinct mechanistic pathology -- the clinical failure is the ABSENCE of a dedicated disengage operation, not excessive goal value. This argues REE needs an explicit, separable abandon/disengage trigger rather than tuning MECH-292's recoverability/persistence weights; an inhibition-only or weight-tuned design reproduces exactly this failure.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The disengagement here is ATTENTIONAL (working-memory focus shifting), not goal-level commitment revocation; the mapping is structural analogy (a disengage trigger whose absence is itself the pathology), not mechanistic identity. Integrative review, not a single empirical test.
mapping.source_context: Cognitive-science integrative framework synthesising attentional-control and executive-function evidence in depressive rumination (human clinical/subclinical).

confidence_components.notes: Canonical, heavily-cited primary statement (high source quality). Mapping fidelity moderate-high for the structural claim (failure = missing trigger), lower for mechanistic identity (attentional vs goal-level). Transfer risk moderate.
